classObjective: Construct and interpret box plots and histograms to compare the shape, center, and spread of two data sets.
workedExamples: 9-6, Example 3 — Compare Two Data Sets with Graphs and Statistics
9-6, Example 4 — Compare Two Data Sets Using Box Plots and Histograms
warmUp: Compare the spread of two data sets given their ranges: Set A range = 20, Set B range = 35
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: comparing distributions using box plots and histograms; choosing appropriate comparison statistics based on distribution shape
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice comparing two data sets using visual displays and statistics
independentPractice: Practice problems comparing two data sets using box plots, histograms, and appropriate statistics
assessment: Exit ticket: compare the test scores of two classes using given box plots — describe differences in shape, center, and spread
capReflection: What visual elements are most helpful when comparing two data sets side by side?
